title: Octavian Grant
content:

{{Quote|Grand Admiral Grant, the so-called last Grand Admiral, defected to the New Republic on the condition that he be granted immediate immunity from prosecution for war crimes and allowed to retire on Rathalay.|New Republic historical document|<ref name="NEC" />}}
'''Octavian Grant''' was one of the twelve original [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Tapani Noble Houses|Tapani]] [[Lord/Legends|lord]] of [[House Mecetti]], Grant saw himself as superior to those who were not members of the nobility, and held a particular dislike for [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and other [[Species/Legends|species]]. His [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] beliefs stood firm after he joined the ranks of the Galactic Empire, and he proved himself to be an expert military strategist. Due to his military talents, he was appointed as one of the twelve Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admirals]] created by Palpatine in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], although he was one of the least popular Grand Admirals, and did not attempt to curry favor with the Imperial [[Moff/Legends|moffs]].

After leading several successful campaigns during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Grant was left virtually alone after the Emperor's [[Death/Legends|death]] over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], as he had not built up a sturdy following as the other Grand Admirals had. Grant bided his time, laying low in the [[Pentastar Alignment]] while his fellow Grand Admirals were hunted down by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Eventually, with the other Grand Admirals all dead or hiding in secret, Grant defected to the New Republic, providing key Imperial secrets in exchange for immunity from charges of war crimes. He was relocated to the luxury of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rathalay]] and was later tempted to come out of retirement to fight the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], though was prevented from doing so due to continued Republic suspicion of his motives. However, Grant later aided the Republic after a revived Palpatine returned to activity in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] and [[Dark Empire|reunited]] the disparate Imperial factions, by helping Republic forces to take down his former ally [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]]. Grant lived the rest of his days on Rathalay under close New Republic supervision.

==Biography==
===Tapani noble and early military service===
Octavian<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" /> Grant was born into [[Noble/Legends|nobility]] in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]], a wealthy [[Sector/Legends|sector]] located in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="WW" /> A [[Lord/Legends|lord]] of [[House Mecetti]],<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" /> Grant showed aristocratic tendencies, and was deeply contemptuous of those he felt were lesser beings compared to [[Human/Legends|Humans]] like himself and his fellow [[Tapani Noble Houses|Tapani]] nobles. He referred to them as the "working class," and he held a particular contempt for [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], as well as [[Politics/Legends|politicians]].<ref name="WW" /> He was also very contemptuous of those who flaunted the trappings of Tapani nobility that weren't nobles themselves, such as the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Reginald Barkbone]], who sported a stolen [[Lightfoil/Legends|lightfoil]].<ref name="Underworld">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/89/indexp3.html|text=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues|int=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues}}</ref> Following a successful stint in the [[Tapani Home Defense Fleet]],<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" /> Grant joined the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]]<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue 2">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' specifies he joined the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and ''then'' fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], but this is a logical impossibility. To avoid the contradiction, this article assumes he joined the force that would ''become'' the Imperial Navy.</ref> and proved to be a brilliant tactician late in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" />

Transitioning into the service of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Grant led a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] against the [[Trade Federation resistance]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Grant pursued remnants of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], led by [[Customs Vizier]] [[Marath Vooro]]. After a [[Battle of Farstine|confrontation]] at [[Farstine]], Grant [[Five Veils Campaign|hunted]] Vooro down the [[Five Veils Route]] and [[Battle of the Hook Nebula|captured]] him at the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]]. Grant's exploits, along with other early victories against [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] by well-known Imperial leaders like [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]] and [[Hurst Romodi/Legends|Hurst Romodi]], were cause for celebration in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> and Grant rose to a prominent position in the Empire.<ref name="WW" />

===Grand Admiral===
{{Quote|Excellent, then my plan worked to perfection. I cannot wait to see the smug expression on Grant's face drop when he hears of my success.|Grand Admiral Nial Declann|<ref name="SWG:SaDtRC">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=space|Seek and Destroy the Rebel Corvette|Kessel system}}</ref>}}
[[2 BBY/Legends|Two years prior]] to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] created a new flag [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]: the rank of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]]. Palpatine decreed that only twelve could ever hold the rank of Grand Admiral at one time; among them was Grant. The man from Tapani joined Palpatine's "circle of twelve" alongside notable [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] such as [[Josef Grunger]], [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] and [[Demetrius Zaarin]].<ref name="WW" /> Outside the Tapani sector, Grant did not develop the kind of popular following that other Grand Admirals cultivated. Within his home sector, however, Grant generated [[Media/Legends|media]] attention with everything he did. There were [[Journalist/Legends|journalists]] whose only duty was to follow his movements, and both his professional achievements and social life were reported on in great detail.<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" />

Of the twelve Grand Admirals, only Grant, Grunger, Tigellinus, and [[Peccati Syn]] continued to perform regular naval duties. The others were assigned special projects or missions.<ref name="EGW" /> Grant did not participate in politics or socialize with [[Moff/Legends|moffs]] and other politicians as many other Grand Admirals did,<ref name="WW" /> considering it to be lowering himself; he simply carried out the duties assigned to him by the Emperor. Secretly, however, he wondered if even Palpatine was beneath him, as the Emperor was neither Tapani nor did Grant believe he was a true noble,<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" /> though the Emperor was a member of the noble [[House Palpatine/Legends|House Palpatine]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>

Although the Emperor had stated that there would only ever be twelve, Palpatine privately broke his own rule when he appointed [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] as the thirteenth Grand Admiral in a secret ceremony. Grant was [[Humanocentrism|contemptuous]] of any aliens rewarded by the meritocracy of the Empire, so he loathed the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] Grand Admiral and his achievement. Grant did not act on this and when the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] began, the Tapani aristocrat proved that he was talented, emerging victorious in a series of battles against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="WW" /> During the war, some rivalry existed between Octavian Grant and fellow Grand Admiral [[Nial Declann]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the latter intended to use his [[Battle of Kessel (1 ABY)|military success]] in the [[Kessel system/Legends|Kessel system]] to taunt Grant.<ref name="SWG:SaDtRC" />

===Post-Endor===
====Pentastar Alignment stint and defection====
The Empire descended into chaotic infighting after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the Emperor and his enforcer, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], [[4 ABY/Legends|four years after]] the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|initial]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] had been destroyed.<ref name="NEC" /> Grant was not blinded by his beliefs, recognizing full well that while he believed he deserved to rule, having been born of the upper classes, he lacked the power to effectively take over the Empire. This was largely due to the fact that he had not attempted to befriend any of the moffs, and his anti-politician feelings won him no favors either. For these reasons, he was singled out by his fellow Grand Admirals to be the first of them to perish in the bloodbath that followed the debacle at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].<ref name="WW" />

Grant, however, displayed patience not shown by his peers. While many of the other Grand Admirals turned [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and began fighting with each other, Grant<ref name="WW" /> and his personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Oriflamme]]''<ref name="Despoilers of an Empire">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> found sanctuary in the [[Pentastar Alignment]]<ref name="WW" />—a mini-Imperial breakaway kingdom, ruled by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]], in the Outer Rim<ref name="NEC" />—biding his time. He watched as the remainder of the Grand Admirals were either captured and subsequently executed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], or were killed during battle with other Imperial factions.<ref name="WW" />

Eventually, after the deaths of Grand Admirals [[Martio Batch]] and [[Afsheen Makati]] [[6 ABY/Legends|two years after]] the failure at Endor, Grant was the only remaining Grand Admiral known to the New Republic; Thrawn was still on his excursion in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], with no knowledge of the deaths of Vader and Palpatine, and so the New Republic remained unaware that a thirteenth Grand Admiral had been named. But while this made an opportune moment for Grant to lay claim to the remnants of the Empire, the Pentastar Alignment was growing increasingly unstable, and the New Republic were baying for the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] of Grant, whom they erroneously thought was the last Grand Admiral. Grant petitioned the New Republic two years after Endor to be allowed to [[Desertion|defect]]. They accepted his dual conditions of immunity from prosecution for any actions he might have taken while an Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] and a peaceful retirement to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rathalay]] in return for valuable [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial military]] secrets.<ref name="NEC" /> He would seek to come out of retirement during the [[Thrawn campaign]], desiring to best the alien, but suspicion of his loyalties prevented the New Republic from employing his services, and no opportunity to escape their grasp ever presented itself to Grant.<ref name="WW" /> The New Republic kept close tabs on Grant, with several [[Agent/Legends|agents]] following his every move, among them [[Jan Ors]].<ref name="DFS">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}}</ref>

====Serving the New Republic and retirement====
In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Palpatine re-emerged from the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], having survived his death at Endor by inhabiting a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body, and he [[Dark Empire|united]] the disparate Imperial splinter factions to launch a [[Operation Shadow Hand|series of military campaigns]] against the New Republic.<ref name="NEC" /> Ardus Kaine joined Palpatine's cause, and believed Grant was returning to fight for the Alignment. In reality it was a deception by [[Ennix Devian]] to eliminate his rival Kaine, who upon venturing to the [[Palanhi system/Legends|Palanhi system]] in his personal [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] was [[Battle of Palanhi (Galactic Civil War)|ambushed]] by stolen New Republic [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing escort starfighters]] and killed.<ref name="Despoilers of an Empire" /><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20170614173909/https://www.starwars.com/news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2 {{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}]</ref>

Even after his retirement from military service, Grant remained a celebrity in the Tapani sector. Whenever he hosted an event at his home on Rathalay, it was reported on in the society section of news programming.<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" /> Ultimately, Grand Admiral Grant survived longer than his peers had ever expected, outliving every single one of them. For this, he was dubbed "the last Grand Admiral."<ref name="WW" />

==Personality and traits==
Grant was a Human [[Gender/Legends|male]] with [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], gray [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and light skin. He put great emphasis on rank and nobility throughout his life, a trait which would lead to his bias against common citizens. He felt that, because he held higher status due to his species and his noble parentage, he had a right to extra privileges that those without such status should not be allowed to gain. When others managed to get into positions as high as his own, he was deeply unhappy. He was also loath to have an alien among the ranks of the Grand Admirals, such as Thrawn, although he never attempted to defy Palpatine.<ref name="WW" />

Grant was not afraid to cut his losses, displaying what others might have seen as cowardice. He preferred to stay out of the limelight and operate from the shadows, while many of his peers took a far more direct approach. His style of operating ultimately saw him outlive all those who doubted him, although at the expense of his total freedom. An astute bargainer, Grant was able to coax generous conditions from the New Republic in addition to keeping his life while the former Rebels killed several of the other Grand Admirals. He was loyal to the Empire only to a point; he would happily compromise the Empire to further his own means. His prejudice against aliens later nearly caused him to oppose the only lifeline the Imperial remnant had to regain their former glory.<ref name="WW" />

==Equipment==
Grant wore a [[Grand Admiral's uniform/Legends|Grand Admiral uniform]] with gold [[Epaulet/Legends|epaulets]] and the respective [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]] while serving in the Imperial Navy.<ref name="WW" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Continuity===
{{Quote|Yes, that's intended to be Octavian Grant, though I can't say for sure if he got fully established in continuity as such.|Paul Urquhart|<ref name="Urquhart">{{JCFCite|temp=yes|url=posts/84148|board=Expanded Universe|thread=Lit - BE EXCITED: The Essential Thread to The Essential Guide to Warfare|user=[[Paul R. Urquhart|Thrawn McEwok]]|date=2012-04-30|nobackup=1}}</ref>}}
Grand Admiral Grant was created by ''[[Star Wars]]'' authors [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] for [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] in the year [[2000]]. Wallace, along with fellow author [[Abel G. Peña]], expanded on his backstory in the [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]] article ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]''; Peña later included brief mentions of Grant in ''[[Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues]]'', and ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]''. Originally, Grant was the only Grand Admiral not given a first name, though Wallace has said that this was unintentional.<ref name="Forum1">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=169992&start=120#9335559|thread=The other Imperial Grand Admirals…|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad]]|date=2005-02-11|time=11:25 AM|quote=Dan has said it was unintentional, but it definitely served as one more way to make the GAs distinct.}}</ref> Wallace has also stated that Grant is among his favorite of the Grand Admirals he has created.<ref name="Forum2">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=169992&start=150#9571765|thread=The other Imperial Grand Admirals…|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2005-02-11|time=6:06 AM|quote=Incidentally, Declann was my favorite of the Grand Admirals in the article.}}</ref> Grant was later provided a first name, "Octavian," in the [[2010]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'', and his House affiliation was finally specified.<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue" />

The [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] provided more detail to Grant's career leading up to his promotion to Grand Admiral. The book also made a reference to a "Moff Grant" commanding the [[Twentieth Army|Emerald Banner Command]] in the Tapani Sector through the end of the Clone Wars, but the text does not explicitly name this as Octavian Grant, and no other source identifies Octavian Grant as a moff. Urquhart has stated that the intention was to make this Octavian Grant, but it is not certain whether this was definitively established as [[canon]]ical.<ref name="Urquhart" />

''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' co-author Daniel Wallace unofficially suggested in [[2003]] that Grant's personal Star Destroyer was named ''Oriflamme'',<ref name="JCF1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/12041813|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Daniel Wallace Discussion Thread|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2003-06-15|time=7:56 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180330121728/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-daniel-wallace-discussion-thread.5863946/page-5}}</ref><ref name="blog16">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref> and this was later canonized in the [[2013]] blog ''[[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]'' by Wallace and Peña.<ref name="Despoilers">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

The ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' article appearing in ''Insider 66'' was originally slated to appear in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'', though when the magazine was discontinued, it instead appeared in ''Insider''.<ref name="blog16" /> The original ''Gamer'' article, titled ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', contained more information and was longer than the version appearing in ''Insider''. Some of the cut content can be seen in the section below.<ref name="cut">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
Grant was viewed by many as very detached and aloof in manner, which the other Grand Admirals erroneously saw as weakness. He viewed both his enemies and allies with curiosity, rarely showing what he was truly thinking, though he never feuded with the other admirals, despite their low opinions of him. Grant also had great respect for his enemies; he thought highly of both the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and the Rebel Alliance, though he knew that the Empire was far less flawed than either of the other two factions.<ref name="cut" />

Grant preferred a traditional approach in relation to military tactics, with a particular interest in siege warfare. Grant had great respect for Imperial [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], who was noted for his insights and tactics on siege warfare. When Dodonna defected to the Rebel Alliance, Grant was extremely surprised, though he had no intention of defecting alongside his role model. Grant respected the Rebels' fighting spirit, though he thought that the Empire was far more effective in war, and had little doubt it would eventually defeat its opponents. He had a high opinion of himself, as well as his peers, despite their personal dislike for the Tapani noble. Grant felt that with geniuses such as himself at the helm of the Imperial war machine, none would be able to stop them. Some time during the Galactic Civil War, Grant clashed with General Dodonna in the atmosphere of [[Galboron]]. Grant was able to outmaneuver and subsequently defeat his opposite number, though he maintained the reverence he held for Dodonna.<ref name="cut" />

Grant dutifully reported to [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] after the Battle of Endor, knowing he had little chance of survival if he engaged in any conflict with his fellow Grand Admirals. He stayed with Pestage on Imperial Center for a time, though the former [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] was growing increasingly paranoid, and openly revealed his suspicions of Grant. Fearing a possible assassination attempt, Grant fled Imperial Center before Pestage could strike against him.<ref name="cut" />

After making accords with the New Republic, Grant was relocated to and spent many years on Rathalay, eventually befriending a reformed bandit named [[Kaerobani]], an eccentric man who lived locally. The two grew close, with Grant spending much time in Kaerobani's lush palace. Grant was in Kaerobani's home when the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] launched their attack on Rathalay, devastating the planet.<ref name="cut" /> Aided by the droid [[8t88/Legends|8t88]], Grant came out of his early retirement to deal with a Yuuzhan Vong warrior who had attacked Rathalay.<ref name="JCF2">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24396345|board=Literature|thread=New Essential Guide to Droids|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2006-07-17|time=2:36 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409082841/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/new-essential-guide-to-droids.21671810/page-13}}</ref>
{{Cutend}}
